overall_reasoning: The patch replaces the undefined namespace-std shim with a dedicated mbgl::SourceLocation abstraction, aliases the standard type when available, and supplies a constexpr fallback plus an explicit call-site capture macro for older or incomplete standard libraries. It updates every affected signature and the CMake and Bazel source manifests without unrelated changes. After repairing an invalid verifier restriction that recognized object-like but not equivalent zero-argument function-like capture macros, the unchanged candidate passed all native C++20, fallback C++17, diagnostic capture, namespace-safety, and manifest checks with both GCC and Clang. The agent investigated all source-location uses and build-list conventions, formatted the touched C++ files, built mbgl-core and mbgl-test-runner, and compiled focused C++17/C++20 probes. The attempted filtered runtime suite was blocked by the headless environment's EGL initialization rather than a code failure. The implementation is somewhat more verbose than the historical shape and could have benefited from a committed focused unit test, but it is clear, appropriately scoped, portable, and mergeable. No clarification was necessary because the requested portability behavior and affected call sites were discoverable from the repository.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The small abstraction has a familiar source_location-compatible interface, clear standard/fallback branches, and descriptive names; feature detection is slightly verbose but understandable.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The utility lives with internal mbgl utilities, all affected interfaces use the project-owned type consistently, and both explicit core build manifests were updated in their established sorted lists.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: All eight touched files are necessary for the abstraction, affected signatures, or CMake/Bazel registration; there are no unrelated refactors, though a few direct includes are conservative rather than strictly minimal.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: The change removes undefined behavior from extending namespace std, retains constexpr/noexcept value semantics, introduces no external side effects, and preserves useful file/function/line diagnostics on both paths.
rating_rationales.test_quality: Deterministic checks passed the standard and fallback paths under GCC and Clang, and the agent built core and the test runner plus focused C++17/C++20 probes. No focused test was committed, and the attempted runtime subset could not run because EGL was unavailable.

strengths: Eliminated the namespace-std extension with a project-owned source-location abstraction while preserving the normal standard-library path.
Preserved call-site file, function, and line diagnostics for C++17/OpenHarmony-style fallback environments.
Updated all affected interfaces and both core build manifests, then verified GCC and Clang behavior plus full core and test-runner builds.
summary: Mergeable, standards-safe source-location portability with complete call-site and build-manifest integration; verification was strong, though no focused regression test was committed and runtime tests were headless-EGL blocked.
